case.focal_claim: The average plumbing firm whose name begins with A or a number receives more service complaints than other firms.

execution_overview.overall_summary: Executed both tasks in Python within Docker after fixing dependencies and file paths. Results consistently show that firms with names beginning with 'A' or a number receive more complaints, supporting the focal claim.

deviations.description: Updated analysis_info.json to include patsy and compatible statsmodels; added in-script pip install fallback.
deviations.reason: Initial execution failed due to ModuleNotFoundError for statsmodels.

deviations.description: Implemented robust search for final_data.dta instead of relying on a single hardcoded path.
Added a small writer to save outputs to /app/data/outputs/task1_results.json in addition to stdout.
Resolved minor code insertion issues while adding dependency handling.
deviations.reason: Edits introduced syntax duplication; subsequently cleaned.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed planned path (t-test, logit, Poisson, ZIP, negative binomial with controls). Only dependency and path robustness changes were made.

test_statistics.other: From NB model with controls: complaints_2008 ~ first_A + ad_spend_k + firm_age + chicago + emp_size + multiple_names

result_raw.result_text: Negative binomial regression with controls shows a positive and statistically significant association: firms with names starting with 'A' or a number have more complaints.

conclusion.conclusion_text: The controlled negative binomial model indicates higher complaint counts for 'A/number'-starting firms, supporting the focal claim.

deviations.description: Added in-script pip install fallback for statsmodels and patsy.
deviations.reason: Ensure library availability inside container runtime.

deviations.description: Removed a hardcoded reassignment of DATA_PATH to enable robust path detection.
deviations.reason: Hardcoded override caused FileNotFoundError in the container.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed the planned comparable path: Welch t-test and NB regression of complaints_2008 on first_A.

result_raw.result_text: Negative binomial model shows a large positive and highly significant effect of first_A on complaints; Welch t-test also indicates higher mean complaints for first_A firms.

conclusion.conclusion_text: The comparable NB test and Welch t-test both support that firms with names starting with 'A' or a number receive more complaints.
